text.title: Inside CHINA... (Not What You’d Expect) 🇨🇳
text.u: we're coming to Beijing we are
brother sister
yes after recently reopening from 3
years of covid isolation we are heading
to the People's Republic of China a
country we hear so much about but one
that most of us know so little of
China's often dubbed One of the most
difficult countries to travel to and
within with a difficult Visa policy the
lack of western-based apps we've all
become completely accustomed to when
traveling and difficulties using VIs and
MasterCard but there's actually a way to
enter China without a Visa and a
passport and with that in mind we hopped
on a plane and flew to Beijing thank you
byebye
bye welcome to Beijing airport welcome
to China it is our first time here in
China we haven't quite made it yet we've
still got passwort control to go through
and we're arriving here trying to take
advantage of the 144-hour Visa free rule
that there is for both British citizens
and Russian citizens and
that's available if you're flying into
Beijing and out of Beijing to another
country again which we are so um yeah
hopefully everything goes right at
passport control and we're going to be
in Beijing we're in China oh my
goded we actually only had to fill in a
few short forms and have a chat with
immigration and share our onward travel
plans and they stamped our passports it
all took around a couple of hours and
then we were through it seemed like we
were the last people leaving the airport
but we finally made it and we can say
welcome to China The Next Step was to
get a cab into the City and Uber grab
Bal Maxim Yandex or any of the taxi apps
we're used to are completely useless in
China so we downloaded an app called daa
travel and I'm not going to lie it took
us an awfully long time to figure it out
because everything is obviously in
Mandarin which we don't speak but we got
there in the end once we sat in the taxi
we were greeted by probably the World's
friendliest cabie and despite the
language barrier and some confusion with
how to accept the ride on the app our
journey went relatively
[Music]
smoothly that was an easy taxi ride it I
made it I can't believe
it so we landed at 2:30 and the time now
is
6:00 um you know it took us maybe an
hour or so to sort out all the documents
of immigration and then just all the
rest of the time trying to sort out the
taxi into the city we could have got a
train but then we would have still had
to get a taxi from the main train
station to here so we just thought taxi
all the way and here we are 3 and 1/
half hours later but we've arrived
that's the main
thing good morning last night we were
pretty ZK to be honest after getting in
it was a long long travel day it wasn't
supposed to be it was supposed to be a
3-hour flight and then a quick nip into
Beijing Center but everything's a little
bit more complicated when you arrive
here in China the usual apps that we're
used to when we're traveling most of the
world don't work so you need to download
new apps sign up to them most of them
are only in Chinese which makes it a
little bit more difficult you also have
to sign up to Ali pay in order to pay
for anything with your foreign card but
once we' sorted all that out we got into
to the city and yeah we're were a little
bit tired so we went out for a little
walk a little look around and um yeah
we're up bright and early this morning
to um go and explore Beijing I still
can't believe we're here to be honest
it's absolutely unbelievable I'm so over
the moon so looking forward to seeing a
bit of China you know what I'm really
looking forward to as well starting our
day with you guessed it picking chicken
nope picking
[Laughter]
dock
a tram it does look like a tram I
thought at first it was a
tram so if Vietnam was full of
motorbikes China is full of normal bikes
here in Beijing when we were driving
into the City yesterday everyone was
either on little electric scooters or
bikes and um provides a totally
different vibe to be honest um it's a
lot quieter a lot calmer than than Hanoi
yesterday everything is just so
interesting and fascinating
here move China down in the real China
[Music]
Town fifth
floor
thank
you huge
so if we read right this is supposed to
be one of the oldest restaurant chains
here in Beijing and one of the best
places to try peing duck and already
when we saw the chefs just come past a
minute ago with the duck it smelt
absolutely incredible and looked even
better I cannot wait to try it I'm so so
excited it's interesting to feel like
everyone knows what they're doing like
what they ordering
and we have not for clue what's going on
Lost in the
corner lost in the corner kind of
true the duck looks insane it smells so
good the juice is just dripping
down and on to my mouth
too last you talking about
to sounds so
gross this pastry is so thick but it
holds so well it
doesn't
do
[Music]
guys we just tried picking dog in
[Music]
picking
[Music]
it's a really really strong flavor of
green tea you know when you leave your
tea bag there for too long in your cup
this is what it is in an ice cream
form Jasmine green tea wow that is so
unusual let's head to the main
Square mhm let's
go one first impression of Beijing
considering we're in the very center
it's nowhere near as busy as I thought
it was going to be I don't know why
again it's just a stereotype you think
in China you're going to be cramped up
shoulder to- shoulder there's just going
to be people everywhere but there's so
much space face it seems like there's
hardly anyone out on the streets and
yeah you can just you can walk freely
between the three of you talk in a line
and yeah it's not crowded at all we're
in the center of the capital right now
it's actually Sunday today as well
supposed to be the busiest day it's not
you can't say it's not busy it is but
not how we expected it not crowded not
crowded I didn't expect the roads to be
that wide and that big it feels really
spacious there were 10,000 scho in
Beijing and a couple of Security checks
later welcome to tianan square a place
that I've seen a thousand times on the
news on different travel videos a place
that we've dreamed about coming for a
long long time it feels so surreal to be
here to be standing on tenanan Square
looking at the big photo of chairman ma
looking at all the monuments all the big
buildings all around here
incredible absolutely
incredible it's even bigger in real life
as well than it looks on the TV on the
camera which to be honest doesn't
usually happen usually you get to a
place and it's a lot smaller than it
looks on camera but yeah this is
massive wow
incredible in the very heart of
China
so big so I just said it's often the
case that you see something on TV so
many times or on different videos and it
looks so much bigger than it actually is
in real life but but here it's the
opposite yeah yeah yeah it's absolutely
Grand in
sky so as um foreigners we need to carry
our past passports with us everywhere
and to get into the square you need to
go for a few Security checks like they
check your bags and they check your
passports and like everyone goes through
but I guess Locos have this ID cards or
something like that but we have to carry
our passports with us we learn that last
night when we walked into the square in
the evening um we didn't have our ports
who we were trying to show our pictures
uh our pictures we were trying to show
pictures all possible on our phones and
it was fine but he was like next time
just make sure you bring a passport so
it
did walking through the middle of tanan
square and seeing all of the internal
tourists as well that here a lot of
people here with suitcases that are
obviously passing through Beijing it's
hard to really grasp the size of China
both in terms of area and obviously
population and there's so many different
faces that you see from different
regions of China as well it's just
incredible and yeah to be right here in
the heart of Beijing and to see lots of
people that have come here from all over
the
country yeah
incredible I don't know as well despite
this being the first day of four here in
China I'm already kind of sad we're
leaving and I feel like I really want to
go off to other regions of the country
to see how different people live
different foods different cultures it's
so so varied there's so many different
cultures and types of people and
religions and histories and yeah we're
leaving in 4 days we going have to come
back we need to get Visa and come back
we get Visa and come back de we got the
little taste of it we like it come
[Music]
back
[Music]
[Music]
there's definite Soviet Vibes in this
main Square here in Beijing I was
expecting a lot of similarities between
China and the former Soviet Union
obviously with the history over the past
80 years or so but to be honest this
main square area around here is a lot
more similar than I was expecting and
when we were driving in yesterday a lot
of the streets the apartment blocks the
size of the roads it's incredibly
incredibly similar to Moscow for example
St Petersburg a lot of former Soviet
cities that we've been to if it wasn't
for the Chinese writing you'd think we
could be in Moscow we all said it last
night when we were walking around that
if it wasn't for Chinese riding is
everywhere and people in general
we think that we're in
Moscow it's very strange I didn't I
didn't expect that we all said that we
didn't have any sort of expectations
from China but we didn't expect it to be
that way if that makes sense does it
does it make sense it makes
sense
it is unbelievable here and also by the
way this is the first time in I think 3
months that we've put trousers and
jackets on it is such a weird feeling to
not be sweating and boiling hot but this
square is just absolutely incredible
it's so big honestly the scale of it is
just
[Music]
unfathomable we are all so in love with
the weather I don't know like I tried I
tried so hard to be fine with this heat
and you know not to pay attention not
overthink it but now it's like the
perfect weather it's sunny and it's
Breezy it's so comfortable to walk
around I just feel alive feel
alive
honestly at every turn there's something
else we feel like we've left the square
and we were heading out of the very
center and then there's another Street
another walking Street and actually on
this walking Street we're on here
there's old tram tracks so I'm guessing
the old tram used to run through the
city right here into the main tianan
Square so beautiful isn't it so
colorful I love all this intricate
details there the color of it
[Music]
all
we just saw this as we were walking down
the street and they s them everywhere
candid fruits and guess what this one is
I don't actually know the name in
English we'll write it below we'll write
it below
uh crunchy on the outside yeah it's so
interesting so crunchy and the
combination of sweet and savory so
delicious I can see why they sell them
everywhere
here have to be careful with the Pips
though so many of
them so
nice
well we said earlier it wasn't that busy
it seems like we found the crowds
now this street is full of all sorts of
foods all sorts of smells from all
directions it is incredible lights
everywhere love
it that looks really interesting as well
what is
it
[Music]
[Music]
yeah
hello nice meet you how are you meet you
we're coming to Beijing thank you very
much we brother what country are you uh
England and Russia oh England and Russia
Russia yes we are brother we sister yes
I agree we come to Beijing thank you
thank
you byebye see you see
you imagine nearing that all
day just get used to it we've only
walked past twice and it's already done
my head
in just get used to it I don't know yeah
maybe Fade Out it's like living under a
flight path or something isn't it that
looks interesting
like a steamed
cake
try let's
try still have absolutely no idea what
it
is it's rice in the sweet
syrup it's quite chewy and gloopy and
it's not what I I don't know why I it's
going to be sour is it a similar text
similar texture to Moi or no
no it's similar to rice
pudding m a little flavor I think but
it's a little more blend yeah it's not
the sweetness comes from the
sauce but itself this thing is not sweet
this
thing my expert opinion this thing does
not taste very
[Music]
[Applause]
sweet so we just got one more snack from
the street we were just on we were just
walking right towards the exit and then
we saw like a group of people outside of
one of the stores and they were buying
this which are beef patties with like a
pastry that is incredibly crispy and
yeah let's give it a
going
M it's like a
burger really peppery lots of spices but
wrapped in a pastry that's really nice
with the meat inside being like a a
minced meat the texture is quite soft
but then the pastry on the outside it a
totally different texture it's lovely
the crunchy the soft peppery ever so
slight Spice in there it's really juicy
as well that's lovely glad we got that
12 Yuan so that's about a pound just
over a pound
amazing see one thing I wanted to add on
to the end of talking about that bun was
it's really interesting how they have
similar types of meat pies and buns all
the way along what was the former Silk
Road and obviously Xian was the end of
the Silk Road on the Chinese start or
the start of the Silk Road um but yeah
it's interesting to see the influence
here in Beijing and the pies are similar
to the pies you see in Central Asia in
the caucuses in turkey and all the way
into Europe I love it we're so far away
but also there's so many
similarities what a day first
impressions as we said a lot of times
today of Beijing are only incredibly
incredibly positive we've had an amazing
day seen some amazing things tried some
amazing things spoke to some incredibly
friendly people but uh time's getting on
and we have to wake up really early
tomorrow morning because you're going to
see something special so we're going to
leave you here so thanks for watching
this video and see you in the next
[Music]
one
